feat: migrate worship songs to GraphQL (#617)

* feat: migrate worship songs from Firestore reads to GraphQL

- Add worshipSongs query and addWorshipSong/updateWorshipSong/
  deleteWorshipSong mutations to the GraphQL schema
- Add worshipSongs.ts resolver reading from Firestore; mutations verify
  auth via context.uid (Firebase ID token in authLink)
- Replace window.__worshipSongs bridge with Apollo useQuery/useMutation
  in useWorshipSongs.ts
- Update WorshipWidget to use useQuery(GET_WORSHIP_SONGS)
- Change WorshipSong timestamps from Firestore objects to ISO strings
- Remove getWorshipSongs() from restoreUserData (Apollo handles caching)
- Remove window.__worshipSongs global from firebase.ts and window.d.ts
- Run codegen to regenerate packages/shared/src/apollo/generated.ts
- Update all affected tests (1657 passing)

commit.message:
fix: prevent Smart Tool Routing from overriding Model Router

The Smart Tool Routing was force-upgrading to gpt-4.1-mini whenever
tools were present and provider wasn't 'openai' or 'anthropic'.
This overrode the Model Router's quality-first selection of Grok 4.1
Fast (#1 tool calling) via OpenRouter.

Fix: Add all our routed providers (openrouter, groq, google, moonshot)
to RELIABLE_TOOL_PROVIDERS since the Model Router already selects
tool-capable models. Also prevent Cost Router from overriding the
Model Router's quality-first decision unless budget is exceeded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix GitHub Pages blank page with conditional base path

Use GITHUB_PAGES env var to set base path only for GH Pages builds,
keeping root path for Vercel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: sync lift type and reasons from server on judge join/reconnect

Side judges were reading liftType from their local state (defaulting to
'squat') instead of the server's session settings on join, causing them
to show reasons for the wrong lift. The same bug caused a reconnecting
judge to miss lift changes made by the head judge while they were
disconnected.

On join/reconnect, all clients now restore settings (liftType,
showExplanations, requireReasons) directly from the session_state
delivered in join_success. The server is the single source of truth;
the head judge's localStorage is no longer read or written for these
settings, eliminating cross-session bleed (e.g. deadlift cached into a
new squat session).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Thermal Type Glitch — fullscreen effect editor

Single-page HTML/CSS/JS animation with glassmorphic editor panel.
28 controllable parameters across structure, motion, color, and effects.
Persistent slice targets with 7 distinct easing curves.

commit.message:
fix: ORC behavioral violations — remove self-serve web search, add attribution mandate

Root cause: ORC instruction told it to web-search market data itself instead of
routing to MRI. This caused O-01 (specialist work), NS-01 (no provenance),
and U-03 (no KB citation) failures. code_scorer_pass_rate was 54%.

Changes:
- Strengthen PROHIBITED: explicit ban on asserting audience/channel/market from
  general knowledge or web search; ban on market analysis/audience sizing/channel
  allocation without routing to specialist
- RESPONSE FORMAT: replace vague inline source phrases with explicit 8-category
  provenance tag mandate (MCMAP_KB, SPECIALIST_OUTPUT, USER_PROVIDED, etc.)
- Compress ROUTING RULES MRI instruction (saves 86 chars)
- Compress RESPONSE FORMAT concise/research-findings lines (saves 100 chars)
- Net CRLF: 7949 -> 7904 (well within 7999 limit)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add debug log for challenge sending on client

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: production-grade real-time log watcher

Node.js + Express SSE-based log tailing system with:
- Efficient reverse-chunk last-N-lines reader for large files
- Incremental append-only file watcher with rotation/truncation support
- Multi-client SSE broadcasting
- 93 unit and integration tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: HR module with document generation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

V41: simplify terminations to robot_lab style

Remove bad_orientation, base_collision, stagnation, thigh_acc terminations.
Keep only time_out (20s) + terrain_out_of_bounds (8m radius).

bad_orientation conflicted with fallen start (killed before recovery).
base_collision conflicted with fallen start (robot on ground = normal).
stagnation punished not moving before robot learned to walk.

Robot_lab (anymal_d) also disables illegal_contact, relying purely on
reward shaping for behavior guidance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ml): cap validation days at 20% of data span

With 75 days of data and VALIDATION_DAYS=30, the split was 57/43 —
far too little training data. The fixed 30-day window was designed for
2 years of data (30/730 = 4%), but kicks in at only 60 days.

Fix: cap validation at max(7, data_span * 20%) so it never exceeds 20%
of total data. Result with 75 days: 15d validation (~80/20 split).
With 2+ years: still 30 days (4%, unchanged).

commit.message:
Add glossary standardization with LLM deduplication and document backlinks

Migration 011: adds canonical_id (self-referential FK) and source_document_ids
(JSON list) to glossary_terms for tracking merged terms and their sources.

Backend:
- GlossaryService.standardize_glossary(): fetches all terms, sends to LLM to
  identify synonym/duplicate groups, sets canonical_id on non-canonical terms,
  stores merged source_document_ids on canonical terms
- GlossaryService.list_canonical(): returns deduplicated terms with source
  document titles for each, using source_document_ids or fallback to document_id
- POST /glossary/standardize: triggers LLM grouping, returns merge stats
- GET /glossary/canonical: paginated canonical list with source doc info
- New schemas: GlossaryCanonicalOut, GlossaryCanonicalListOut, StandardizeResponse

Frontend:
- Glossary view toolbar: All Terms / Standardized toggle + Standardize button
- All Terms view: each term shows a "source" button linking to its document
- Standardized view: merged terms show multi-source badge + pill buttons per
  contributing document, all clickable to open the document modal
- Event delegation on glossary-list for source doc clicks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Links section to README

commit.message:
Replace MCP server with static AI skill, add theme extracts

Remove the MCP server (mcp/) which required hosting infrastructure on
Fly.io and replace it with a build-time generated AI skill. The skill
is a single SKILL.md file that teaches AI models how to fetch and
analyze the published JSON data via public URLs — zero infrastructure.

- Add `build-skill` CLI command that scans databases and generates
  SKILL.md with current docket metadata, data schemas, and query
  guidance
- Integrate skill build into GH Pages pipeline
- Add AI Skill section to landing page with link to SKILL.md
- Add footer to dashboard with AI Skill link
- Export theme-extracts.json (per-comment, per-theme extracted
  positions, concerns, recommendations, quotes) in website build
- Add theme extract display in dashboard comment cards

commit.message:
feat(ml): occupancy dropout training to improve future predictions

The model learned park_occupancy_pct (15%) as the dominant feature,
making hour (0.84%) nearly irrelevant. This is correct for real-time
predictions but wrong for future predictions (tomorrow, next week)
where only the historical DOW×hour profile is available.

Fix: Occupancy Dropout during training — 30% of training rows have their
actual park_occupancy_pct replaced with the DOW×hour historical mean
(computed from the same park's training data). This teaches the model
to also rely on hour/day_of_week/seasonal features when occupancy is
approximate, matching the inference scenario for future predictions.

- config.py: OCCUPANCY_DROPOUT_RATE = 0.30 (configurable, 0 = disabled)
- features.py: apply dropout in add_park_occupancy_feature training mode

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Session 166: Fix Heartland adapter — switch to subdiv_results.cgi (ALIVE)

subdiv_results.cgi was incorrectly marked DEAD in Session 87.2. Deep
re-investigation (Session 166) confirmed it returns individual match
results with dates, team IDs, and scores — the Heartland equivalent
of GotSport's &date=All fix.

- Switch scrapeCGIResults() from subdiv_standings.cgi to subdiv_results.cgi
- subdiv_standings.cgi only returned W-L-T aggregates (no individual matches)
- subdiv_results.cgi returns per-match rows: date, game#, home+ID, score, away+ID, score
- parseResultsHtml() already expected this 7-column format — no parser changes needed
- Also discovered seedings.cgi goldmine (complete team roster with IDs)

This should recover ~640 Heartland orphan teams (96.4% had zero match data scraped).

commit.message:
fix(worker): cap retry countdown to visibility timeout across all tasks (#720)

* fix(worker): cap bundle analysis retry countdown to task visibility timeout

Prevents retry countdown from exceeding TASK_VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
which could cause tasks to become invisible in the queue before being retried.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(worker): Clamp bundle analysis retry countdown between floor and ceiling

Extract retry countdown clamping into a helper `_clamp_retry_countdown`
used by both retry paths in BundleAnalysisProcessorTask. The countdown
is bounded between a floor of 30s and a ceiling of
TASK_VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS - 30 (870s in production).

The floor prevents a near-zero countdown when the visibility timeout is
small (e.g. in tests or low-timeout environments). The ceiling ensures
the ETA always fires before Redis can redeliver the task from `unacked`,
with a 30s buffer instead of the previous 1s.

Fixes CODECOV-59
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(worker): Ensure retry countdown ceiling never falls below floor

When TASK_VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS is small (e.g. in low-timeout
environments or tests), TASK_VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS - 30 could be
less than the floor of 30s, causing the floor to silently override
the ceiling and making the ceiling meaningless.

Compute _RETRY_COUNTDOWN_CEILING as max(visibility_timeout - 30, floor)
so the two bounds are always consistent. Also removes the mocker patches
from the clamp tests — they're no longer needed since the ceiling is now
well-defined in all environments.

Refs CODECOV-59
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(worker): Make retry countdown floor relative to visibility timeout

Previously the floor was a hardcoded 30s, which could exceed the
visibility timeout in low-timeout environments (e.g. 20s test env),
meaning even the floor could trigger redelivery from unacked.

Floor is now min(30, visibility_timeout // 2), so it scales down
proportionally for small timeouts while staying at 30s in production.

Examples:
  900s visibility → floor=30s, ceiling=870s (unchanged)
   20s visibility → floor=10s, ceiling=10s
   10s visibility → floor=5s,  ceiling=5s

Refs CODECOV-59
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ref(worker): Move clamp_retry_countdown to base and apply across all tasks

Move the clamp_retry_countdown helper from bundle_analysis_processor to
tasks/base.py so every task that schedules retries is protected from
setting a countdown that exceeds the Celery visibility timeout.

Any countdown longer than TASK_VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS causes Redis to
redeliver the task from unacked before the ETA fires, creating duplicate
executions that multiply exponentially. Clamping to [floor, ceiling] where
both bounds are derived from TASK_VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS prevents this.

Applied to: notify, manual_trigger, http_request, upload_processor,
bundle_analysis_notify, bundle_analysis_processor, test_analytics_notifier,
test_results_finisher.

Refs CODECOV-59
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(worker): apply clamp_retry_countdown to upload pipeline tasks

preprocess_upload, upload_finisher, and upload were missed in the
previous commit. All three had unclamped LockRetry countdowns, and
upload.py additionally takes max(lock_countdown, exponential_backoff)
before scheduling the retry — compounding both unbounded sources.

Refs CODECOV-59
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ref(worker): cap LockManager countdown at visibility timeout and move tests

Replace the hardcoded 5-hour cap in LockManager with
TASK_VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS - 30, so LockRetry.countdown is
bounded at the source rather than relying solely on call sites to
clamp. Tasks still wrap with clamp_retry_countdown as a safety net.

Move TestClampRetryCountdown from test_bundle_analysis_processor_task
to test_base where it belongs.

Refs CODECOV-59
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(worker): clamp remaining LockRetry countdowns in test_analytics_notifier

Lines 165 and 212 were missed in the previous pass. All three LockRetry
handlers in this file now consistently wrap e.countdown.

Refs CODECOV-59
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ref(worker): Move clamp_retry_countdown into BaseCodecovTask.retry()

Clamping at the override ensures every self.retry() call is safe without
requiring each call site to wrap its countdown. Removes all per-site
clamp_retry_countdown() calls and their imports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(worker): fix countdown clamping and test for visibility timeout cap

- Fix `countdown or 0` bug in BaseCodecovTask.retry() that was changing
  parameterless self.retry() calls from Celery's default 180s to 30s;
  now only clamps when countdown is explicitly provided
- Update test_locked_exponential_backoff_retry_2 to assert countdown
  equals MAX_RETRY_COUNTDOWN_SECONDS since retry_num=2 always exceeds
  the visibility timeout cap (1800s uncapped > 870s cap)
- Fix ruff formatting in test_analytics_notifier.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ref(worker): name retry countdown magic numbers and add explanatory comments

Replace bare `30` literals with named constants to clarify intent:
- `TASK_RETRY_COUNTDOWN_BUFFER_SECONDS` (shared/celery_config.py) — the
  safety margin subtracted from the visibility timeout ceiling, now shared
  between base.py and lock_manager.py instead of duplicated
- `_RETRY_COUNTDOWN_FLOOR_SECONDS` (tasks/base.py) — the minimum retry
  delay, renamed for clarity

Add comments explaining there is no hard requirement for these values and
noting that our backoff is ~6× more aggressive than Celery's built-in
default_retry_delay (180 s).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ref(worker): deduplicate retry countdown ceiling into celery_config

Move TASK_RETRY_COUNTDOWN_FLOOR_SECONDS and TASK_RETRY_COUNTDOWN_CEILING_SECONDS
into shared/celery_config.py so both tasks/base.py and services/lock_manager.py
import them rather than each computing the same expression independently.

base.py no longer needs TASK_VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS since all derived
constants now live in celery_config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ref(worker): rename FLOOR/CEILING to MIN/MAX and tighten comments

Rename TASK_RETRY_COUNTDOWN_FLOOR_SECONDS -> TASK_RETRY_COUNTDOWN_MIN_SECONDS
and TASK_RETRY_COUNTDOWN_CEILING_SECONDS -> TASK_RETRY_COUNTDOWN_MAX_SECONDS for
clarity.

Clean up comments: drop the fragile 6x ratio (would go stale if the backoff base
changes), remove the redundant MAX constant description (the expression is
self-explanatory), and use short BUFFER/MIN labels instead of repeating full
constant names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(worker): update stale "5 hours" comment in lock manager test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(worker): fix stale cap assertion in lock manager test

Replace hardcoded 18000 (old 5-hour cap) with MAX_RETRY_COUNTDOWN_SECONDS
and strengthen the assertion from <= to == since retry_num=10 always hits
the cap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ref(worker): derive clamp_retry_countdown cap from hard_time_limit_task

The 30-second buffer in clamp_retry_countdown was arbitrary — no test
could assert it must be 30 rather than any other value.

The principled bound is: a task calling retry() may have been running
for up to hard_time_limit_task seconds, so the safe countdown ceiling is
TASK_VISIBILITY_TIMEOUT_SECONDS - hard_time_limit_task. This matches the
same logic already used in get_lock_timeout().

Move clamp_retry_countdown from a standalone function to a method on
BaseCodecovTask so it can read self.hard_time_limit_task. Falls back to
TASK_RETRY_COUNTDOWN_MAX_SECONDS (visibility timeout - 30 s) for tasks
with no configured hard limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: remove extra blank lines left by clamp_retry_countdown deletion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(worker): guard hard_time_limit_task against MagicMock in tests

When tests patch BundleAnalysisProcessorTask.app with a MagicMock,
self.app.conf.task_time_limit returns a MagicMock, which clamp_retry_countdown
then tries to compare with > 0, raising TypeError.

Add isinstance(hard_limit, int) check so non-int values fall through
to the TASK_RETRY_COUNTDOWN_MAX_SECONDS fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(worker): address code review feedback on retry countdown clamping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(worker): enforce retry countdown floor and fail loudly on misconfigured tasks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove stale lock-manager-incr-expire-atomic-fix note

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove stale lock-manager-sentry-counter-flood note

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(worker): drop retry floor, rename constant, add hard_time_limit_task docstring

clamp_retry_countdown is a pure ceiling function — enforcing a floor is
the caller's responsibility. Rename TASK_RETRY_COUNTDOWN_MIN_SECONDS to
TASK_RETRY_MIN_SAFE_WINDOW_SECONDS to reflect its actual role as the
threshold below which the safe window is too small to retry safely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(cancel): Bestätigungs-Email an eingeloggten User statt localStorage-Cache

- customerEmail kommt jetzt aus useAuth() (aktuell eingeloggter User)
- Vorher: localStorage-Cache konnte veraltete Email eines anderen Accounts enthalten
- authUser.email hat Priorität über userData.email aus localStorage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Load pending challenges on connect so they show in notification bell

Challenges were only delivered via real-time WebSocket events, so if the
recipient wasn't online when the challenge was sent (or refreshed), they
would never see it. Now the server indexes challenges by recipient in
Redis and includes them in the friends_list initial payload.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
